V-M-037.checks: Admin frontend must become compact and usable from a phone for operational status, user search, devices, payments/analytics, and system health.
V-M-037.test-files: frontend-admin/src/components/Layout.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Users.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Devices.tsx frontend-admin/src/pages/Analytics.tsx frontend-admin/src/components/StatCard.tsx frontend-admin/src/index.css frontend-admin/src/lib/api.ts frontend-admin/src/types/index.ts
V-M-037.commands: cd frontend-admin && npm run build; Phase-24 mobile admin smoke for /, /users, /devices, and /analytics at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932; assert scrollWidth <= innerWidth and device actions require confirmation

module-checks.check-2: Mobile overview surfaces service health, users, subscriptions, revenue, VPN/system status, and urgent counts without desktop-only layout.
module-checks.check-3: User search and device registry are readable on phone viewports without table-only primary workflows.
module-checks.check-4: Device block, unblock, rotate, and revoke are reachable and require confirmation.
module-checks.check-5: Audit-sensitive admin actions preserve current backend audit expectations and show refreshed success/error feedback.
module-checks.check-6: No deploy/install scripts are modified during Phase-24 execution.

viewport-matrix.viewport-1: Overview loads without horizontal document scroll and exposes system health plus urgent user/device navigation.
viewport-matrix.viewport-2: Search, counters, role/status, identity, and pagination fit without a desktop-only table dependency.
viewport-matrix.viewport-3: Device rows, signals, and block/unblock/rotate/revoke confirmation paths fit within the viewport.
viewport-matrix.viewport-4: Payment and analytics status remains readable without charts dominating the first phone viewport.

scenarios.scenario-1: Admin can inspect system state and manage a device from a phone without switching to desktop layout.
scenarios.scenario-2: Admin can search a user and read role/status/account context from compact rows at 360px width.
scenarios.scenario-3: Admin can open a device action confirmation that names the target peer before mutation.
scenarios.scenario-4: Critical device actions are hidden in a wide table or unavailable on mobile.
scenarios.scenario-5: An admin action lacks confirmation or loses audit visibility.
scenarios.scenario-6: A compact UI change modifies deploy/install scripts, backend business rules, or billing provider behavior.

planned-evidence.evidence-1: Record npm build result after source changes.
planned-evidence.evidence-2: Record Chrome/CDP or equivalent viewport smoke for /, /users, /devices, and /analytics.
planned-evidence.evidence-3: Record static review that block, unblock, rotate, and revoke cannot execute without confirmation.
planned-evidence.evidence-4: Record git diff check for deploy, install.sh, docker, and nginx surfaces.
